title: Whats with the general reaction to EoE
display_title: Whats with the general reaction to EoE
wikitext:
[]: What's with the general reaction to EoE?             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Mon Sep 08, 2008 9:48 pm Why do I get the feeling that all the people calling it the archetypal "Kill 'Em All" ending didn't pay attention to the movie worth jack shit? Sure, everyone dies horrible deaths, but Shinji basically pressed a colossal reset button by rejecting Instrumentality. If Asuka can come back, so can everyone else who died during the movie.

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Mon Sep 08, 2008 10:11 pm Too late, this is a topic no one wants to go into. SSD made a thread of "don't go there topics" and this was one of them. It has been talked to death.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 09, 2008 1:32 am ^^ The search function is your friend. I don't think we have anyone here espousing the 'archetypal "Kill 'Em All" ending' interpretation (and I say that as someone who took a very negative interpretation away).

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Tue Sep 09, 2008 2:33 am The best way to look at it once kom susser tod starts there is so much ambaguity in what actually happens nobody can be wrong with what is said to have happened (within reason). My interpritation of what happened would be different from your interpritaion of what happened the same with SSD, Mr Tines, Reichu ect ect. Thats how I see it anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by master_lloyd » Tue Sep 09, 2008 12:00 pm OFN. On a related note did you guys hear about Rei being a clone?!

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Tue Sep 09, 2008 3:26 pm Action_Bastard wrote: Too late, this is a topic no one wants to go into. SSD made a thread of "don't go there topics" and this was one of them. It has been talked to death.

[]:             Quote Post by Zeak » Tue Sep 09, 2008 3:51 pm SeanTucker wrote: ...he/she did?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Tue Sep 09, 2008 5:22 pm SeanTucker wrote: Action_Bastard wrote: Too late, this is a topic no one wants to go into. SSD made a thread of "don't go there topics" and this was one of them. It has been talked to death.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Tue Sep 09, 2008 5:32 pm Oh. I wasn't really trying to add anything new relating to discussion of the scene itself, I was just commenting on how people regard EoE as the quintessential "everyone dies" ending even when that's obviously not the case to anyone who actually pays attention. I think I worded the thread title wrong; changed it to reflect this.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Sep 09, 2008 6:22 pm Tines: Thanks for clearing that up. Anyway at topic: I'd say it's because people just aren't paying attention to what the show is saying.

[]:             Quote Post by CorporalChaos » Tue Sep 09, 2008 6:26 pm master_lloyd wrote: On a related note did you guys hear about Rei being a clone?!

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Tue Sep 09, 2008 6:29 pm CorporalChaos wrote: master_lloyd wrote: On a related note did you guys hear about Rei being a clone?!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 09, 2008 7:45 pm I heard that the Evas aren't really robots, but I need sauce on this.

[]:             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Tue Sep 09, 2008 7:49 pm My point is that EoE being a "kill 'em all" ending is a misconception that an alarming number of people have, even people who claim to have watched it and paid plenty of attention. "Rei = clone" and "Evas =! robots" are both concepts that ANYONE who's seen the series, whether they paid attention or not, would have gotten. And I know no one on HERE espouses that opinion, but elsewhere on the Internet (TVTropes, GameFAQs, 4chan, 7chan) I've seen people accusing Anno of trying to "out-Tomino the man himself".

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Tue Sep 09, 2008 8:05 pm SeanTucker wrote: My point is that EoE being a "kill 'em all" ending is a misconception that an alarming number of people have, even people who claim to have watched it and paid plenty of attention.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Tue Sep 09, 2008 9:59 pm well I call it a "kill'em all" ending in the sense of just how shocking it is that every character dies, while at the same time conscious of the fact that "anyone can come back" and Third Impact was a reset button (....which is important for Rebuild of Eva)

[]:             Quote Post by Lucretius » Tue Sep 09, 2008 10:01 pm V is about to get lynched for mentioning the sequel theory. (Which has been unfairly maligned, IMO).

[]:             Quote Post by Action_Bastard » Tue Sep 09, 2008 10:03 pm I apologize for my previous post, please disregard it.

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Wed Sep 10, 2008 1:46 am SeanTucker wrote: I've seen people accusing Anno of trying to "out-Tomino the man himself".

[]:             Quote Post by AyrYntake » Wed Sep 10, 2008 2:01 am SeanTucker wrote: I've seen people accusing Anno of trying to "out-Tomino the man himself".
summary: []: What's with the general reaction to EoE?             Quote Post by SeanTucker » Mon Sep 08, 2008 9:48 pm Why do I get the feeling that all the people calling it the archetypal "Kill 'Em All" ending didn't pay attention to the movie worth jack shit? Sure, everyone dies horrible deaths, but Shinji basically pressed a colossal reset button by rejecting Instrumentality. If Asuka can come back, so can everyone else who died
